steps.title: Identify the coefficient of a
steps.content: In $x = 8a(b+9)$, the variable $a$ is multiplied by the term $8(b+9)$. To isolate $a$, we divide both sides by this term.

steps.title: Divide and isolate a
steps.content:
Dividing both sides by $8(b+9)$:
$$\frac{x}{8(b+9)} = \frac{8a(b+9)}{8(b+9)}$$
The right side simplifies:
$$a = \frac{x}{8(b+9)}$$
This matches choice B — B is correct.
